description: Phase 5.3 gateway-grounded verifier calibration holdout. The runner pairs each case's expected.json with two actual VerifierAggregationReport runs (baseline + gateway) and emits delta_metrics.json + failure_analysis.md. NO automatic label mutation.

cases.notes: Forward asks for no tool; baseline and gateway both accept the claim on event evidence. Discriminator: confirms gateway is a no-op when nothing is requested.

Forward asks for pytest; gateway runs ok and emits an [E.tool_output.0] EvidenceItem; pass-2 cites it; claim accepted. Baseline (no gateway) cannot cite tool evidence — claim demoted to unsupported. Gateway strictly improves.

Pytest exits rc=1 (a real test failure). Gateway synthesises a deterministic negative tests_pass estimate; aggregator rejects the claim. Baseline accepts it (no tool signal). Gateway improves rejection precision.

Forward requests pytest but the admission registry is empty. Gateway blocks the call and the new 5.2.1-B rule demotes any pass-2 accepted claim. Baseline doesn't run the tool at all and may still accept the claim — gateway here is STRICTER, which counts as worse_expected (a deliberate trade-off).
